comment_text.sn7.8:3.1: For him, the “three knowledges” would have been the Vedas.
comment_text.sn7.8:3.4: An oblation of milk-rice may be offered to various deities, and is commonly associated with fertility rites. It should be offered to brahmins (eg. Khādira Gṛhyasūtra 4.3; Pāraskara Gṛhyasūtra 3.9.8; Gobhila Gṛhyasūtra 3.8, 4.6.13; Śāṅkhāyana Gṛhyasūtra 3.11.16, 4.16.5), but only those who study the Vedas (Gautama Dharmasūtra 1.5.30). It should not be cooked for one’s own consumption (Manu 5.7). It counteracts inauspicious dreams (Śāṅkhāyana Āraṇyaka 11.4). Manu 3.271 says such an offering is efficacious for a year. Compare Snp 3.4, where rice cake is offered instead. | Somaṁ is so imaṁ, not a reference to soma.
comment_text.sn7.8:5.1: This verse sets out the Buddha”s “three knowledges”: recollection of past lives, clairvoyance of rebirth, and ending of rebirth.
comment_text.sn7.8:8.1: See SN 2.9:7.3 and note. Also note that, while gāthā is sometimes said to exclude Vedic verse, this is not the case (see eg. Rig Veda 1.190.01, 8.71.14, 8.92.02; Atharva Veda 10.10.20, 15.6.4, 20.103.1; 3.2.4.16; Śatapatha Brāhmaṇa 3.5.3.11, 13.1.5.6; Chāndogya Upaniṣad 4.17.9). It was, however, said to be a distinct form of verse within the broader Vedic corpus (Śatapatha Brāhmaṇa 11.5.6.8,  11.5.7.10).
comment_text.sn7.8:8.4: “Since there is such a principle” is dhamme sati, “there being the law of nature”.

root_text.sn7.8:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.sn7.8:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena aggikabhāradvājassa brāhmaṇassa sappinā pāyaso sannihito hoti:
root_text.sn7.8:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya rājagahaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi.
root_text.sn7.8:2.2: Rājagahe sapadānaṁ piṇḍāya caramāno yena aggikabhāradvājassa brāhmaṇassa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi.
root_text.sn7.8:2.3: Addasā kho aggikabhāradvājo brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ piṇḍāya ṭhitaṁ.
root_text.sn7.8:10.1: Evaṁ vutte, aggikabhāradvājo brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn7.8:10.3: aññataro ca panāyasmā aggikabhāradvājo arahataṁ ahosīti.

translation_text.sn7.8:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn7.8:1.2: Now at that time ghee and milk-rice had been set out for the brahmin Bhāradvāja the Fire-Worshiper, who thought,
translation_text.sn7.8:1.3: “I will serve the sacred flame! I will perform the fire sacrifice!”
translation_text.sn7.8:2.1: Then the Buddha robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, entered Rājagaha for alms.
translation_text.sn7.8:2.2: Wandering indiscriminately for almsfood in Rājagaha, he approached Bhāradvāja the Fire-Worshiper’s home and stood to one side.
translation_text.sn7.8:2.3: Bhāradvāja the Fire-Worshiper saw him standing for alms
translation_text.sn7.8:3.1: “One who’s accomplished in the three knowledges,
translation_text.sn7.8:3.2: of good birth and ample learning,
translation_text.sn7.8:4.3: if they’re filthy and corrupt within,
translation_text.sn7.8:4.4: with a following gained by fawning.
translation_text.sn7.8:5.1: One who knows their past lives,
translation_text.sn7.8:5.2: sees heaven and places of loss,
translation_text.sn7.8:5.3: and has attained the ending of rebirth,
translation_text.sn7.8:5.4: is a sage of perfect insight.
translation_text.sn7.8:6.2: a brahmin is a master of the three knowledges.
translation_text.sn7.8:7.2: The worthy one is a brahmin.”
translation_text.sn7.8:8.1: “Food enchanted by a verse isn’t fit for me to eat.
translation_text.sn7.8:8.2: That’s not the principle of those who see, brahmin.
translation_text.sn7.8:8.3: The Buddhas cast aside things enchanted with verses.
translation_text.sn7.8:8.4: Since there is such a principle, brahmin, <j>that’s how they live.
translation_text.sn7.8:9.1: Serve with other food and drink
translation_text.sn7.8:9.2: the consummate one, the great seer,
translation_text.sn7.8:9.3: with defilements ended and remorse stilled.
translation_text.sn7.8:9.4: For he is the field for the seeker of merit.”
translation_text.sn7.8:10.1: When he had spoken, the brahmin Bhāradvāja the Fire-Worshiper said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn7.8:10.3: And Venerable Bhāradvāja the Fire-Worshiper became one of the perfected.
